name: Fresh Vegetable Stuffing (from Chicago Tribun
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Spinach Leaves, washed, stemmed and shredded
2 cups Homemade Bread Crumbs (made from 3 or 4 slices of bread)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add carrots and sauté about 2 minutes until lightly golden.
2. Add onions, celery, and all seasonings. Cook for 4 minutes,
stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are slightly softened.
3. Add squash, spinach, and white wine. Cook for an additional
2 minutes, allowing the wine to evaporate slightly.
4. Remove from heat and transfer the mixture to a large bowl.
5. Stir in bread crumbs and parmesan cheese until combined.
6. If using as stuffing, let cool completely in the refrigerator
for at least 2 hours before preparing the turkey or chickens.
7. If baking separately, preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Butter a 3-quart casserole dish and place the mixture inside.
8. Bake until heated through, about 20-25 minutes.
This recipe is designed for stuffing one 14-pound turkey or two roasting chickens. For different sized poultry, adjust the quantities accordingly using the scaling feature of your preferred cooking app.
